data.title: BITF, ABTC, other crypto-tied stocks lead week's financials movers
data.description:
Crypto-focused firms were among the biggest winners and losers across the broader financials space in the week ended Oct. 17.

Bitfarms (BITF), rising the most of any financial stock (with market cap over $2B) this week, surged 19% as the crypto miner named a new CFO to lead financial strategy for its North American AI expansion;

Hut 8 (HUT), another bitcoin miner focused on AI infrastructure, jumped 11%; 

Alternative asset manager DigitalBridge Group (DBRG) climbed 10.6%;

Cipher Mining (CIFR) also gained 10.6%; and

American Express (AXP) rounded out the five biggest weekly gainers with a 9.6% advance, after the credit card lender and network raised the lower end of its 2025 guidance after better-than-expected Q3 earnings.

Conversely, American Bitcoin (ABTC), a bitcoin mining company backed by U.S. President Donald Trump's oldest sons, Eric Trump and Donald Trump Jr., was the biggest financial loser, tumbling 20%;

Baldwin Insurance Group (BWIN) dropped 19.5%;

Crypto exchange Gemini Space Station (GEMI) gapped down 16%;

Chinese financial services firm Lufax Holdings (LU) retreated 12%; and 

Blockchain-based lender Figure Technology Solutions (FIGR) slid 9%.

data.title: Gucci owner Kering is said to be nearing deal to sell Beauty unit to L’Oréal
data.description:
• Kering (OTCPK:PPRUF), the French luxury product maker behind famous brands like Gucci, is in discussions to sell its beauty business to its local rival L’Oréal (OTCPK:LRLCF) (OTCPK:LRLCY), The Wall Street Journal reported on Saturday, citing people familiar with the matter.
  • A potential transaction is expected to be announced as early as next week, assuming the talks won't fall apart and there won’t be another bidder, the people said, adding that the deal could fetch a valuation of roughly $8B for the unit.
  • The report comes only weeks after Kering’s (OTCPK:PPRUY) new chief executive, Luca de Meo, a former CEO of French carmaker Renault (OTCPK:RNLSY) (OTCPK:RNSDF), assumed the company’s leadership in September.

data.title: Paramount Skydance reportedly speeding up mass layoffs
data.description:
Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY) plans to lay off around 2,000 of its employees starting from the final week of October, accelerating a prior timeline to begin mass layoffs in November, Variety reported on Saturday.

The job cuts are set to begin on Oct. 27 as an initial round of layoffs, impacting the company’s U.S. as well as overseas employees. They are expected to continue throughout the year, according to Deadline, which first reported the news.

Soon after Skydance Media closed its $8B merger with Paramount Global in August, David Ellison, chairman and CEO of the newly formed entity, announced plans to realize $2B in cost savings, and there were reports that the company was targeting a massive round of layoffs by early November.

Paramount’s latest 10-K filing says that roughly 18,600 full-time and part-time employees serve the company across 32 countries. Skydance has more than 500 workers in two continents, according to the company website.

data.title: Notable analyst calls this week: Nvidia, T-Mobile and WMG among top picks
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SP500) closed in the green on Friday, while Nasdaq (COMP:IND) and Dow (DJI) fell 0.6% and 0.3%, respectively. Wall Street had a slew of upgrades and downgrades from analysts. Here are some of the major calls for the week:

Nvidia, AMD receive bullish views from HSBC

HSBC upgraded Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) to Buy from Hold noting that it expects the AI graphics processing unit total addressable market to keep increasing beyond hyperscalers, leading to continuous earnings growth.

Analysts led by Frank Lee said they upgraded the stock on the back of increasing fiscal 2027 estimate for GPU TAM relative to their previous expectations, leading to an upward revision in their fiscal 2027 data center revenue estimate to $351B, which remains 36% above consensus forecast of $258B.

The brokerage increased PT on Nvidia's stock to $320 from $200.

HSBC also increased PT on AMD's (NASDAQ:AMD) stock to $310 from $185 while maintaining its Buy rating on the shares. Lee and his team said AMD's OpenAI deal implies massive AI revenue upside potential, which is still underestimated by consensus. The analysts believe this deal gives enhanced visibility into AMD’s AI GPU revenue opportunity till 2030.

T-Mobile upgraded on subscriber growth expectation

RBC upgraded T-Mobile US (NASDAQ:TMUS) to Outperform from Sector perform, on expectations the company would see strong subscriber growth in the near term compared to wireless peers and benefit from the US Mobile acquisition in the medium term.

The research firm noted that T-Mobile continued its strong customer acquisition activity in the wireless market. TMUS price target was unchanged at $270.

"While we expect subscriber growth in the industry to slow down sequentially, T-Mobile is well-positioned in a high-churn environment," RBC said.

HP upgraded on valuation, business momentum

HSBC upgraded HP (NYSE:HPQ) to Buy from Hold, citing valuation and business momentum.

Analyst Abhishek Shukla said that the market indicates that PC and printer volumes for HP may be better than they had previously expected. The firm also raised PT on the stock to 30 from 28.10.

The brokerage said it now expects HP’s PC volume growth at 5.1% and 6.9% in fiscal 2025 and fiscal 2026, respectively, and added that tariff concerns are overdone.

"HP no longer makes significant imports from China to the US, suggesting limited downside risks from any US-China tariff tensions," said Shukla and his team. 

Warner Music Group upgraded on expectation of growth reacceleration

Wells Fargo upgraded Warner Music Group (NASDAQ:WMG) to Overweight from Equal weight and said the company is poised for growth to re-accelerate and will benefit from strong underlying secular trends.

The research firm attributes the company's current growth to the successful turnaround at Atlantic Records and Warner Records posting all-time market share highs. They believe the underlying improvements of WMG's frontline labels are sustainable.

WMG has a price target of $39, hiked from $34.

Barclays downgraded CyberArk (NASDAQ:CYBR) from Overweight to Equal Weight ahead of its pending acquisition by Palo Alto Networks (NASDAQ:PANW). Meanwhile, Palo Alto Networks was upgraded by BTIG to Buy, citing “greater confidence” in the cybersecurity company’s ability to achieve growth targets calling for 14% total revenue growth.

Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) was in focus after Citi raised its price target to $240 from $200 on Micron and reiterated Buy rating on the memory maker, citing the belief that dynamic random access memory could see “unprecedented” demand from artificial intelligence.

Citi also raised PT on Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) to $415 from $350 after the chipmaker and Microsoft (MSFT)-backed OpenAI announced a collaboration for 10 gigawatts of custom AI accelerators. Analysts led by Christopher Danely said that while Broadcom did not provide an estimate, they believe this deal could be worth $100 billion and $8.00 in EPS over the next few years. 

Albemarle (NYSE:ALB) received a downgrade to Neutral from Buy with a $100 price target at BofA, which said shares are fairly valued following recent strength on the back of a U.S. government investment in Thacker Pass and export restriction headlines on Chinese lithium batteries, news that investors have "misread" as positive for ALB.

data.title: Roche breast cancer drug improves survival of up to 62% in late-stage trial
data.description:
Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) subsidiary Genentech announced on Saturday that its experimental breast cancer therapy, giredestrant, as part of a combination regimen, reduced the risk of disease progression or death in up to 62% of patients in a Phase 3 trial when used as a late-line option.

Citing a presentation selected for a European medical event, the Swiss drugmaker stated that 44% and 62% of intent-to-treat (ITT) and ESR1-mutated populations in its evERA study experienced a benefit in progression-free survival, respectively, after receiving giredestrant plus everolimus, another cancer drug.

evERA was designed to evaluate giredestrant plus everolimus against standard-of-care endocrine therapy plus everolimus as a late-line option in patients with estrogen receptor (ER)-positive, human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 (HER2)-negative breast cancer.

ER-positive breast cancers make up nearly 70% of breast cancer, the second most common cancer type among women.  Up to 40% of those with ER-positive cases are found to have ESR1 mutations following treatments with a cancer drug type called cyclin-dependent kinase (CDK) 4/6 inhibitors.

evERA enrolled patients with ER-positive breast cancer who had previously received CDK 4/6 inhibitors and endocrine therapy with PFS in the ITT and ESR1-mutated populations, its primary endpoints.

Genentech added that there was a clear, favorable trend in terms of overall survival, the secondary endpoint of evERA, but data were immature, and OS data will be followed up through the next analysis.

Adverse events in the on-drug arm were manageable and in line with the known safety findings of individual drugs, the company said, adding that there were no new safety signals.

Genentech said it will discuss the data with health regulators in an attempt to commercialize giredestrant “as soon as possible.”

data.title: Salesforce CEO walks back on San Franciso troop deployment comments
data.description:
Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) CEO Marc Benioff has apologized for comments he made last week suggesting that the Trump administration should send federal troops to San Francisco, where his company is headquartered.

In an interview with The New York Times, the tech billionaire said he supported the government's moves to send the National Guard to Democrat-led cities, including San Francisco, where his company’s annual Dreamforce conference took place this week.

“We don’t have enough cops, so if they can be cops, I’m all for it,” Benioff noted, sparking backlash, including within his circle. The deployment of the National Guard to Portland, Oregon, and Chicago has caused protests and lawsuits, leading to arrests of undocumented migrants and U.S. citizens alike.

On Thursday, The Times reported that prominent venture capitalist Ron Conway, who backed companies such as Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) and Airbnb (ABNB), had stepped down from the Salesforce Foundation board over Benioff’s comments.

In an X post late Friday, he appeared to backtrack from his previous remarks. “Having listened closely to my fellow San Franciscans and our local officials, and after the largest and safest Dreamforce in our history, I do not believe the National Guard is needed to address safety in San Francisco,” Benioff said.

“My earlier comment came from an abundance of caution around the event, and I sincerely apologize for the concern it caused.”

Dear readers, We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: OpenAI 'dominating' consumer AI token consumption, Anthropic wins enterprise: Barclays
data.description:
Recent disclosures from OpenAI (OPENAI) and Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) show how far ahead the ChatGPT maker is ahead of the search giant in the race for consumers. However, Google is not standing still.

OpenAI is winning the consumer battle

At OpenAI's recent developer day, the company announced that its API platform was processing 6B tokens each minute, up 20 times over the past two years. The company also said that ChatGPT had surpassed 800M weekly active users.

In comparison, Google said recently it was processing 1.3 quadrillion tokens each month across all of its services, including AI Overviews, Gemini, and the API platform on Google Cloud. 

“In piecing together these datapoints, we estimate that while OpenAI is dominating consumer AI token consumption (2x+ the size of Google Gemini), Google processes the most tokens out of the AI labs by a wide margin,” Barclays analyst Ross Sandler wrote in a note to clients. “The lead in aggregate inference tokens is likely from the 2B+ search users generating AI Overviews (2B users disclosed on the 2Q25 call and estimated to be closer to 2.5B users currently), accounting for 900T (~70%) of the 1.3Q monthly tokens. The market share of native consumer AI applications likely tips much more towards OpenAI.”

Tokens explained

Tokens are units of data — such as words, parts of words, or punctuation — that are used by language models to process text. They are largely responsible for driving economics in artificial intelligence, including cost and revenue. 

Delving deeper, Sandler estimated that ChatGPT is generated more than 420T inference tokens each month from its 800M weekly users. (He also estimated that the service could have 1.2B monthly users). That is more than double the roughly 190T tokens that Gemini is generating from an estimated 550M monthly users.

On Alphabet's most recent earnings call, the company disclosed the Gemini app had surpassed 450M monthly users, with daily requests up 50% from the prior quarter.

Enterprise is Anthropic's race to lose

While the competition for consumer-generated tokens appears is being led by OpenAI, that's not the case in the enterprise. Anthropic (ANTHRO) is leading by a wide margin. 

Anthropic has “nearly” double the revenue share when compared to OpenAI, as evidenced by recent reporting. The company is on pace to reach an internal goal of a $9B annualized revenue run rate by the end of 2025, and could generate as much as $20B in annualized revenue by the end of 2026.

The company, which launched its Claude chatbot in March 2023, said its revenue run-rate had reached more than $5B as of August. Anthropic also said that it has more than 300,000 business customers and the number of customers that spend more than $100,000 with it had grown “nearly” seven times in the past year.

As such, Anthropic is leading the enterprise space by a “decent margin when it comes to inference tokens,” Sandler estimated.

data.title: Back to the future: The next-gen DeLorean aims to be electric and blockchain-savvy
data.description:
There is still some buzz that a new DeLorean model could be on the roads more than 40 years after it featured in the Back to the Future film trilogy and became entrenched in pop culture.

The original DeLorean Motor Company was an American automaker founded by John DeLorean in 1975. Known for its stainless-steel, gull-wing sports car, the company's rise and fall was dramatic, ultimately leading to a bankruptcy in 1982.

In 1997, Stephen Wynne, a British engineer and car restorer based in Texas, bought the company’s assets, including parts, schematics, and brand materials from the original manufacturer. John DeLorean's estate disputed the transaction for years, arguing that the trademarks and images were not included in the bankruptcy sale. However, a U.S. federal court settlement granted in 2015 Wynne’s DeLorean Motor Company official rights to use the DeLorean name, logo, and associated trademarks in exchange for an undisclosed payment.

Texas-based DeLorean Motor Company is now focused on building electric vehicles. The first planned model, the DeLorean Alpha5, is an electric sports coupe designed by Volkswagen's (OTCPK:VLKAF) Italdesign featuring the brand’s iconic gull-wing doors, a 100 kWh battery pack, and a roughly 300-mile range. The Alpha5 is still in pre-production. Initial manufacturing is expected next year.

 

 

 

Some reports indicate that DeLorean will partner to some degree with General Motors (GM) and its suppliers for manufacturing, although the company has not issued any confirmation.

Notably, the next-gen DeLorean built the world's first blockchain-powered vehicle reservation marketplace, developed on the Sui blockchain, one of the fastest-growing platforms in the space. Slots on the reservation list are tradeable, meaning potential DeLorean buyers can move up or down the waiting list. While the crypto-based system is heavily promoted and enables direct trading of reservation slots, DeLorean has added support for standard payment methods such as credit cards.

The DeLorean brand image still holds some sway in the automobile industry. When Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) unveiled the Cybertruck in 2019, Musk acknowledged that its design was influenced partly by the DeLorean's iconic stainless-steel aesthetic. He has also called the original DeLorean model one of the "coolest-looking cars" ever made.

Looking ahead, if a new DeLorean does make it out of production, the impact on the electric vehicle is expected to be positive. While DeLorean is not anticipated to be any sort of volume player, there could be a halo effect of positive headlines from the EV cool factor. After all, LEGO just unveiled a new Back to the Future DeLorean set, showing the timelessness of the brand.

data.title: Russia-Ukraine de-escalation could push oil prices to $50/bbl, Citi says
data.description:
Crude oil futures ticked higher Friday but posted a third consecutive weekly loss as increased oversupply concerns and further tariff sparring between the U.S. and China sent prices to their lowest in five months.

Prices steadied to end the week after President Trump said he plans to meet with China’s President Xi, likely in two weeks in South Korea, and said his proposed 100% tariff on goods from China was "not sustainable," a notably softer tone that helped turn around Wall Street's early losses Friday.

However, Trump's planned meeting with Russia's President Putin has prompted speculation that a potential easing of sanctions on Russian oil could add to global supply.

A de-escalation of the war in Ukraine could push oil prices toward $50/bbl, Citi senior commodities strategist Eric Lee said in a Bloomberg interview, as a reduced danger of Ukrainian attacks on Russia's refinery network and a relaxation of diplomatic pressure on would-be buyers of Russian crude would "precipitate a faster move" toward the bank's bear-case scenario.

Lee noted that a ~$10/bbl drop would pose a threat to the U.S. shale industry, which requires higher prices than some state-controlled explorers to support drilling activity.

A decline also would raise the issue of whether OPEC+ and de-facto leader Saudi Arabia would revert to defending prices or align with the Trump administration's preference for cheaper oil, Lee added.

Concerns about global oversupply already were strong, and were reinforced earlier this week by the International Energy Agency's reduced demand forecast and increased expectations for a surplus for 2026.

This week, front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for November delivery finished -2.3% to $57.54/bbl and front-month Brent crude (CO1:COM) for December delivery also closed -2.3% to $61.29/bbl; on Friday, WTI and Brent crude gained 0.1% and 0.4%, respectively, snapping a three-day losing streak.

Front-month Nymex November natural gas (NG1:COM) settled -3.1% on the week to $3.008/MMBtu, after rising 2.4% on Friday.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (NYSEARCA:UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG), (NYSEARCA:XLE)

Energy stocks, as represented by the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLE), finished the week +0.9%.

Top 20 gainers in energy and natural resources in the past 5 days: Fluence Energy (FLNC) +40%, Critical Metals (CRML) +39.2%, Stardust Power (SDST) +37.9%, Electra Battery Materials (ELBM) +34.5%, Bloom Energy (BE) +28.3%, Liberty Energy (LBRT) +27.1%, ESS Tech (GWH) +26.9%, Green Plains (GPRE) +23%, Korea Electric Power (KEP) +22.4%, FTC Solar (FTCI) +19.4%, China Natural Resources (CHNR) +19.3%, T1 Energy (TI) +18.7%, Verde Clean Fuels (VGAS) +14.7%, Solaris Energy Infrastructure (SEI) +14.2%, Foremost Clean Energy (FMST) +14%, Stem (STEM) +13.6%, Shoals Technologies (SHLS) +13.6%, Advanced Energy (AEIS) +13.2%, Daqo New Energy (DQ) +12.9%, Central Puerto (CEPU) +12.8%.

Top 10 decliners in energy and natural resources in the past 5 days: Sable Offshore (SOC) -35.8%, American Battery Technology (ABAT) -28.1%, NioCorp Developments (NB) -21.9%, Martin Midstream Partners (MMLP) -18%, IsoEnergy (ISOU) -17.5%, USA Rare Earth (USAR) -14.9%, Gran Tierra Energy (GTE) -11%, Geospace Technologies (GEOS) -10.7%, Amplify Energy (AMPY) -10.6%, Ramaco Resources (METCB) -10.6%.

Source: Barchart.com

data.title: Autoliv targets 3% organic sales growth and $1.2B operating cash flow in 2025 while strengthening China partnerships
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Autoliv, Inc. (ALV) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Mikael Bratt, President and CEO, reported "yet another record-breaking quarter," highlighting significant sales growth driven by robust light vehicle production, particularly in China and North America. He stated, "Our high growth in India continues, accounting for 1/3 of our global organic growth."
  • Bratt emphasized outperformance with Chinese OEMs due to recent product launches and anticipated further outperformance in China for Q4.
  • The CEO announced continued investments in China, including a second R&D center and a new strategic partnership with CATARC to define next-generation safety standards: "Together with CATARC, we aim to define the next generation of safety standards and enhance the safety on the roads in China and globally."
  • Autoliv plans to form a joint venture with HSAE to focus on advanced safety electronics, targeting growth in ECUs for active seatbelts, hands-on detection systems, and steering wheel switches.
  • Bratt highlighted an increase in quarterly dividend to $0.85 per share and reaffirmed share repurchase ambitions of $300 million to $500 million annually.
  • CFO Fredrik Westin stated, "Net sales were approximately $2.7 billion, representing a 6% increase. The gross profit increased by $63 million and the gross margin increased by 130 basis points."
  • Westin added, "Adjusted operating income increased from $237 million to $271 million, and the adjusted operating margin increased by 70 basis points to 10.0%."

Outlook

  • Bratt indicated S&P forecasts global light vehicle production to grow by 2% in 2025, with Q4 production expected to decline by about 2.7%.
  • The company expects to "significantly outperform light vehicle production in China during the fourth quarter."
  • For full year 2025, Autoliv guides for around 3% organic sales growth, adjusted operating margin of 10% to 10.5%, and operating cash flow around $1.2 billion.
  • CapEx guidance was revised to around 4.5% of sales, down from 5%.
  • Bratt stated, "We expect the full year adjusted operating margin to come in at the midpoint of the guided range."

Financial Results

  • Third quarter net sales were over $2.7 billion, the highest ever for a Q3, with growth driven by price, volume, currency, and $14 million in tariff-related compensation.
  • Adjusted operating income rose to $271 million, with a margin of 10%.
  • Operating cash flow reached $258 million, up by $81 million year-over-year. Free operating cash flow was $153 million.
  • Gross margin improved to 19.3%, up 130 basis points from the prior year.
  • Trade working capital increased by $197 million, mainly from higher accounts receivables and inventories.
  • Dividend of $0.85 per share was paid, and $100 million in shares were repurchased.
  • Leverage ratio remains at 1.3x, below the 1.5x target limit.

Q&A

  • Colin Langan, Wells Fargo: Asked why organic sales guidance was unchanged despite a higher light vehicle production (LVP) forecast. Fredrik Westin explained, "Some of these adjustments...are for past quarters...we also see a larger negative mix now after 9 months and also expect that for the full year...some launches in China have been a bit delayed."
  • Langan also questioned strong Q3 margins and one-time items. Mikael Bratt confirmed, "The $50 million is a one-timer...compensation from a supplier for historical cost."
  • Björn Enarson, Danske Bank: Queried Q4 guidance and path to midterm margin targets. Bratt responded, "We feel confident when it comes to our ability to eventually get to our 12% target."
  • Gautam Narayan, RBC: Asked about Q4 headwinds and China market share. Fredrik Westin detailed, "engineering income...is the largest headwind...out-of-period compensation...second largest...depreciation expense increase...third."
  • Michael Aspinall, Jefferies: Explored India growth and the HSAE JV. Bratt noted, "around 5% of our turnover is coming from India...market share in India, 60%."
  • Vijay Rakesh, Mizuho: Inquired about China subsidies and Europe outlook. Bratt said, "We definitely look very positively on China...cautious about the European market more from a demand point of view."
  • Yan Dong, Deutsche Bank: Asked about supplier contract trends and Ford fire impact. Bratt replied, "I don't feel any major concerns around more difficult situation...Ford 150 is around 1% of our global sales."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed management on the sustainability of strong margins, the impact of one-time items, and regional sales guidance, with a generally neutral to slightly positive tone as they sought clarification on operational drivers and strategic progress.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, repeatedly affirming targets and progress, with phrases like "we feel confident when it comes to our ability to eventually get to our 12% target" and "we anticipate strong fourth quarter performance."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts remained inquisitive about tariffs, market mix, and regional performance, while management's tone was consistently confident and focused on operational improvements.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for organic sales growth (3%), adjusted operating margin (10% to 10.5%), and operating cash flow ($1.2 billion) are unchanged, while CapEx guidance improved to 4.5% of sales vs. 5% previously.
  • Strategic focus on China has intensified, with new R&D investment, a partnership with CATARC, and a JV with HSAE.
  • Management tone remains confident, with continued emphasis on outperformance in China, India growth, and cost controls.
  • Analysts’ focus shifted slightly more to region-specific performance and margin sustainability, with recurring questions on mix, one-time items, and guidance clarity.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited temporary headwinds for Q4 including lower engineering income, higher depreciation, and lack of out-of-period inflation compensation.
  • Tariff recovery remains ongoing, with Autoliv still net negative on compensation but expecting full recovery in Q4.
  • Potential supply chain disruptions from a fire at a North American aluminum plant and European production adjustments were noted.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the timing of China launches, supplier contract risks, and the impact of market mix on sales growth.

Final Takeaway

Autoliv’s management reiterated their commitment to achieving full-year targets, emphasizing ongoing investments in China, operational improvements, and cost discipline. The company projects continued outperformance in China, robust cash flow, and shareholder returns, while monitoring short-term headwinds including tariffs, market mix, and regional demand volatility.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
